headline: AMERICAN CASUALTIES IN IRAN WAR — What We Know
summary: Pentagon confirms 13 US service members killed in combat operations, 300+ wounded across 7 countries. IRGC claims far higher numbers — all debunked. EU chief Kallas says Russia is providing Iran intelligence 'to kill Americans.' Congress demands transparency.

items.label: Total US killed (combat-related)
items.detail: 7 killed by enemy fire + 6 killed in KC-135 crash (non-hostile per Pentagon). Sgt. Pennington died from wounds March 8.

items.label: Kuwait Port Strike — March 1
items.detail: Iranian drone struck operations center at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait. All from 103rd Expeditionary Sustainment Command (Des Moines, Iowa). Identified: Capt. Cody A. Khork (35), SFC Noah L. Tietjens (42), SFC Nicole M. Amor (39), Spc. Declan J. Coady (20), Maj. Jeffrey R. O'Brien (45), CW3 Robert M. Marzan (54).

items.label: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington — Died March 8
items.detail: Army Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington (26, Glendale, KY) wounded March 1 at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia. Died from injuries one week later.

items.label: KC-135 Crash, Western Iraq — March 12
items.detail: KC-135 Stratotanker refueling aircraft crashed over western Iraq. Pentagon says NOT caused by hostile or friendly fire — under investigation. Iranian proxy group Ashab Al Kahf claimed responsibility (US denied). Crew included members from 121st Air Refueling Wing (Ohio ANG) and MacDill AFB, FL.

items.label: Non-combat deaths during operations
items.detail: USMC LCpl Kevin Melendez — died March 4 in Saudi Arabia (non-hostile incident). National Guard Maj. Sorffly Davius (46, NY National Guard/NYPD officer) — died March 6 from health-related incident at Camp Buehring, Kuwait.

items.label: Total US wounded
items.detail: Across 7 countries: Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Jordan, Bahrain, Iraq, Israel. 10 seriously wounded, 88% returned to duty. Wound types: burns, traumatic brain injuries, shrapnel. Most injuries from one-way Iranian attack drones.

sections.title: KALLAS / EU — Russia Helping Iran "Kill Americans"

items.label: EU Chief: Russia providing Iran intelligence to target and kill Americans
items.detail: Kaja Kallas (EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs) at G7 meeting in France, March 26: 'Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is also supporting Iran now with the drones so that they can attack neighboring countries and also US military bases.' She linked the wars: 'if America wants the war in the Middle East to stop... they should also put the pressure on Russia.'

items.label: UK Defense Secretary confirms Russia involvement
items.detail: UK Defence Secretary John Healey said the same day he sees the 'hidden hand of Putin' behind Iran's war effort. France's FM Barrot separately confirmed cooperation between Moscow and Tehran. Russia's oil exports doubled to ~$270M/day due to the war.

items.label: IRGC claim: 650 US troops killed/wounded in 2 days
items.detail: IRGC spokesman BG Ali Mohammad Naeini claimed 650 American casualties in first two days, including 160 at US Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain. Pentagon and multiple independent sources disproved. Jerusalem Post and Media Line confirmed this was false.

items.label: IRGC claim: 100+ Americans killed in Dubai
items.detail: IRGC claimed wave of attacks on US bases in Dubai killed 100+ American military and civilian personnel. US State Department called it 'complete disinformation' — no one killed or injured at US Consulate in Dubai.

items.label: IRGC claim: 200+ casualties in single wave
items.detail: IRGC claimed over 200 casualties and 'collapse of Israeli defenses' in a single retaliatory wave. No corroboration from any non-Iranian source.

items.label: Iranian disinformation pattern
items.detail: NewsGuard identified 18 war-related false claims by Iran (vs. 5 in two weeks prior to the war). AI-doctored images and videos used extensively — e.g., fake Burj Khalifa fire video got 100M+ views. Iran's domestic internet at ~4%, controlling the narrative internally.

items.label: Wave 83 — Four US Gulf bases simultaneously
items.detail: IRGC struck Al-Dhafra (UAE), Al-Udeiri (Saudi Arabia), Ali Al-Salem (Kuwait), and Sheikh Isa (Bahrain) simultaneously. First wave to hit all four major US Gulf bases at once. Pentagon has not confirmed any US casualties from this wave.

items.label: Wave 84 — Al-Kharj, US refueling fleet claim
items.detail: IRGC claims it destroyed US aerial refueling aircraft at Prince Sultan Air Base (Al-Kharj), Saudi Arabia, using joint Aerospace Force-Navy missile and drone operation. Claims it penetrated base air defenses with solid- and liquid-fueled missiles. Pentagon and CENTCOM have NOT confirmed or denied.

items.label: IRGC 'Beyond Eye for Eye' Threat
items.detail: IRGC Aerospace Force commander BG Seyyed Majid Mousavi warned: 'This time, the equation will no longer be an eye for an eye — just wait!' Ordered evacuation of workers at all US/Israeli-linked industrial companies across the Gulf. Pattern from prior attacks suggests new strikes imminent.

items.label: House Armed Services Democrats demand public hearings
items.detail: HASC Democrats: 'deeply troubled by the lack of transparency.' Pentagon shifted from 'no casualties' to staged disclosure (3, then 6, then 7, then 13). Base and unit details withheld, citing operational security. Pentagon $200B supplemental request lacks detailed budget breakdown.

items.label: Bipartisan frustration growing
items.detail: Rep. Jason Crow: 'more questions than answers.' Sen. Elizabeth Warren: administration 'cannot explain the reasons that we entered this war.' Sen. Rand Paul: accused administration of changing war rationale daily. GOP lawmakers also 'growing increasingly wary' after DoD briefings left them unsatisfied.

items.label: March 10: ~140 wounded, 8 serious
items.detail: First major wounded count release.

items.label: March 17: ~200 wounded, 10 serious, 180+ RTD
items.detail: 80% returned to duty within days.

items.label: March 24: 290 wounded, 10 serious, 255 RTD
items.detail: 88% returned to duty. Count climbing ~25/week.

items.label: March 27: 300+ wounded
items.detail: Ongoing tally across 7 countries.
